summary:
Alpha-fetoprotein peaks during late first trimester due to high production in fetal tissues and secretion into amniotic fluid. Its maximum concentration is observed in amniotic fluid before transferring to maternal blood.

The summary above contains irrelevant details such as specific references or mention of options ("Based on option C"). Remove these entirely from any final output unless explicitly requested.

Final Summary:  
Alpha-fetoprotein achieves its maximum concentration at the end of the first trimester within amniotic fluid. It then gradually decreases there and enters maternal circulation, leading to rising but lower levels in maternal serum thereafter.
